channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Highway Amenities Struggle to Keep Up with India's Expanding Network
item.description: India’s highway network is growing rapidly, but there is a significant gap in the availability of wayside amenities like rest stops and washrooms. As more people take to the highways for long-distance...

item.title: Top 3 News Stories: R1m Tender Scandal, Joburg Building Collapse, and Teacher Arrested for Lashing Pupil
item.description: In the latest news this morning, a company named Ithani Amen, which was only registered eight days ago, has been awarded a R1 million tender by the national health department, despite not having a bus...

item.title: Government Calls In-Camera Meeting for Parliamentary Leaders on Regional Affairs
item.description: The Pakistani government has called on parliamentarians to attend an in-camera briefing about the regional situation, scheduled for Wednesday. Law Minister Azan Nazeer Tarar confirmed this in a statem...

item.title: Truphena Muthoni breaks world record hugging a tree for 72 hours
item.description: Twenty-two-year-old environmentalist Truphena Muthoni has successfully broken her own world record by hugging a tree for 72 hours straight, surpassing her previous record of 48 hours. The event took p...

item.title: Tanzania Boosts Security for Citizens and Farms Near Wildlife Reserves
item.description: The Tanzanian government has taken significant steps to enhance the safety of its citizens, especially in villages near protected wildlife areas. In an effort to curb the threat posed by wild animals ...

item.title: South Africa’s Economy Shows Signs of Growth, but Challenges Persist
item.description: South Africa’s economy is showing some positive signs, with government debt starting to stabilise and fiscal reforms underway, but it still faces significant challenges. Despite the optimism in the 20...

item.title: Corrections Report for March 2026 by Rappler
item.description: Rappler has issued a correction report for March 2026. The corrections include a clarification on AIRA LABS, a startup whose AI software plays a key role in Naga City's digital operations. This report...

item.title: Bears center Drew Dalman unexpectedly retires at 27 after stellar career
item.description: Drew Dalman, the Pro Bowl center for the Chicago Bears, has announced his retirement at just 27 years old, shocking the NFL community. Dalman had a standout career, playing five seasons in the league....

item.title: The Complex History Behind US-Iran Tensions
item.description: The US-Iran relationship is not a simple tale of good versus evil but a complex, long-standing conflict rooted in mutual fear and misinterpretation. The cycle of tension between these two nations bega...

item.title: B.C.'s Journey to Permanent Pacific Time Year-Round
item.description: British Columbia has been changing its clocks twice a year for over 70 years, but March 8, 2026, will be the last time residents 'spring forward' or 'fall back'. Premier David Eby announced that the p...

item.title: China’s ‘Wolf Warrior’ Diplomacy and Its Confrontations with Filipino Officials
item.description: In the latest chapter of China’s aggressive foreign policy, Beijing's 'wolf warrior' diplomats have openly clashed with Filipino politicians and officials. The Chinese embassy in Manila has been engag...

item.title: S&P 500 Rebounds as Investors Buy the Dip Following U.S.-Iran Conflict
item.description: The S&P 500 ended the day largely unchanged, recovering from earlier sharp declines, as traders took advantage of the dip following recent U.S. and Israeli airstrikes on Iran. The market's recovery wa...

item.title: Khamenei's Death Shifts Attention to Khomeini's Grandson as Possible Successor
item.description: The death of Ayatollah Ali Khamenei in a U.S.-Israeli attack has sparked urgent discussions about his successor as Supreme Leader of Iran. One prominent figure emerging from the late Ayatollah Ruholla...

item.title: The Complex Reasons Behind Leaders' Decisions to Go to War
item.description: War has been a part of human history for millennia, and despite its devastating consequences, it continues to occur. While wars can start for various reasons such as disagreements, fear, or the desire...

item.title: Simple and Affordable Tips to Organise Your Home Efficiently
item.description: Home organisation doesn't have to be costly or overwhelming. Experts explain that the key to a tidy space isn't necessarily about buying expensive storage bins, but about reducing clutter and creating...

item.title: CIA Collaborating with Kurdish Forces to Support Uprising Against Iranian Regime
item.description: The CIA is reportedly working to arm Kurdish forces in Iraq with the aim of sparking a popular uprising in Iran. Sources close to the situation indicate that the Trump administration is engaged in dis...

item.title: UK to Implement Historic Visa Ban to Address Asylum Abuse
item.description: The UK government is set to introduce an unprecedented visa ban targeting nationals from Afghanistan, Cameroon, Myanmar, and Sudan as part of a crackdown on asylum abuse. Home Secretary Shabana Mahmoo...

item.title: Matildas coach stresses empathy for emotional Iran squad ahead of Asian Cup clash
item.description: Ahead of Thursday's Women's Asian Cup group match on the Gold Coast, Iran's striker Sara Didar became visibly emotional while expressing concern for her country and the families of her teammates amid ...

item.title: NASA Reshapes Artemis Mission with New Plans for Lunar Exploration
item.description: NASA has announced significant changes to the Artemis Moon program as it prepares for the historic Artemis II mission, which will include Canadian astronaut Jeremy Hansen. While Artemis III was origin...

item.title: LP Crisis: Former National Organising Secretary Joins Nenadi Usman's Caretaker Committee
item.description: Clement Ojukwu, a former National Organising Secretary of the Labour Party (LP), has expressed regret over the party's poor performance due to the legal battles it has faced since the 2023 general ele...
